listings.title: Apartment, rue Sainte-Apolline (Arts et Métiers)
listings.notes: Original subject. North exposure, busy commercial street. Listing silent on condition. Strong fixtures (moldings/parquet/fireplace). See dvf_analysis.md for the deep dive.
listings.expert_analysis:
**Ask: €2,000,000 for 186.08 m² = €10,748/m².**

Against recent Paris 3rd sales in the 80-250 m² surface band (n=64): median €11,287/m², IQR €9,411–€13,683/m². Asking €10,748/m² → **IN-BAND** — Priced inside the interquartile range — fair for the size class in this arrondissement.

Premium vs median: **-4.8%**.

**Strengths:** elevator, moldings, parquet, fireplace.

**Concerns:** north exposure (less natural light), condition not visible from listing photo, DPE class D (potential future works).

**Notes:** Original subject. North exposure, busy commercial street. Listing silent on condition. Strong fixtures (moldings/parquet/fireplace). See dvf_analysis.md for the deep dive.

listings.title: Apartment, rue Jacob (Saint-Germain-des-Prés)
listings.notes: Heart of Saint-Germain — among the most prestigious addresses in Paris. Small for a 2BR but in a top-tier location. Photo confirms recent renovation with period features preserved.
listings.expert_analysis:
**Ask: €1,700,000 for 83 m² = €20,482/m².**

Against recent Paris 6th sales in the 80-250 m² surface band (n=57): median €16,211/m², IQR €13,118–€19,379/m². Asking €20,482/m² → **ABOVE-BAND** — Above the p75 of recent sales — typical for renovated/exceptional units.

Premium vs median: **+26.3%**.

**Photo signals:** very high ceilings, preserved original casement windows, fresh white paint, premium designer staging.

**Strengths:** elevator, 2nd floor (above street noise), condition: renovated (visible), period building (1700s), 18th-century building, delightful communal areas.

**Notes:** Heart of Saint-Germain — among the most prestigious addresses in Paris. Small for a 2BR but in a top-tier location. Photo confirms recent renovation with period features preserved.

listings.title: Apartment, rue du Bac (Bon Marché)
listings.notes: Listing marketed as 'contemporary loft' but photos show 1970s-era dated finishes. Habitable but will need cosmetic refresh + likely kitchen/bath updates (~€50-150k). 1954 building has no period-Haussmann charm. Double exposure is the genuine plus.
listings.expert_analysis:
**Ask: €1,860,000 for 85 m² = €21,882/m².**

Against recent Paris 7th sales in the 80-250 m² surface band (n=67): median €15,202/m², IQR €12,340–€19,816/m². Asking €21,882/m² → **OVERPRICED** — Materially above the p75 — needs strong condition/floor/view justification.

Premium vs median: **+43.9%**.

**Photo signals:** 1970s-era parquet tile flooring, plain plaster ceiling (no moldings), aluminum-frame sliding windows, visible cable across ceiling.

**⚠ Compounding concern:** asking price is above the band's p75 *and* photos show dated finishes — material renovation budget needs to be priced in.

**Strengths:** elevator, 3rd floor (above street noise), double exposure (East/West (double)), double exposure, marketed as loft-style.

**Concerns:** photos show dated finishes (renovation budget required).

**Notes:** Listing marketed as 'contemporary loft' but photos show 1970s-era dated finishes. Habitable but will need cosmetic refresh + likely kitchen/bath updates (~€50-150k). 1954 building has no period-Haussmann charm. Double exposure is the genuine plus.

listings.title: Family apartment, Europe / Parc Monceau (sectorisé Chaptal)
condition_signals: crisp white repaint, modern outlets installed cleanly
listings.notes: Family-size 3BR in posh 8th near Parc Monceau. The school catchment (Chaptal) is a strong premium signal — appeals to French families. Photo confirms beautifully restored classique with preserved 19th-c. fireplace and panelling.
listings.expert_analysis:
**Ask: €1,930,000 for 144 m² = €13,403/m².**

Against recent Paris 8th sales in the 80-250 m² surface band (n=56): median €12,223/m², IQR €10,177–€15,126/m². Asking €13,403/m² → **IN-BAND** — Priced inside the interquartile range — fair for the size class in this arrondissement.

Premium vs median: **+9.7%**.

**Photo signals:** intact Louis XV marble fireplace, full boiserie wall paneling preserved, Versailles/herringbone parquet in good condition, crisp white repaint, modern outlets installed cleanly.

**Strengths:** elevator, 2nd floor (above street noise), condition: renovated, period charm preserved (visible), period building (1890s), late 19th c. period building, near Parc Monceau, school sector Chaptal.

**Notes:** Family-size 3BR in posh 8th near Parc Monceau. The school catchment (Chaptal) is a strong premium signal — appeals to French families. Photo confirms beautifully restored classique with preserved 19th-c. fireplace and panelling.

listings.title: Apartment, rue de Solférino (Musée d'Orsay)
condition_signals: tall double doors with original trim
listings.notes: First floor with 3.5m ceilings = piano nobile, very desirable. Photo confirms exceptional preserved plasterwork and parquet — appears recently refreshed and move-in ready. Among the strongest condition signals of the set.
listings.expert_analysis:
**Ask: €1,950,000 for 130 m² = €15,000/m².**

Against recent Paris 7th sales in the 80-250 m² surface band (n=67): median €15,202/m², IQR €12,340–€19,816/m². Asking €15,000/m² → **IN-BAND** — Priced inside the interquartile range — fair for the size class in this arrondissement.

Premium vs median: **-1.3%**.

**Photo signals:** elaborate ceiling moldings/rosette intact, full wall boiserie preserved, point de Hongrie parquet visible, tall double doors with original trim.

**Strengths:** condition: renovated showcase Haussmannian (visible), 3.50 m ceilings, stone building (pierre de taille), large fitted kitchen.

**Notes:** First floor with 3.5m ceilings = piano nobile, very desirable. Photo confirms exceptional preserved plasterwork and parquet — appears recently refreshed and move-in ready. Among the strongest condition signals of the set.

listings.title: Apartment, rue de Verneuil / rue de Beaune (Carré des Antiquaires)
listings.notes: Listing claim of 'perfect condition' confirmed by photo. Cleanly refreshed Left Bank apartment with newly finished chevron floors and bright neutral palette — turnkey. 2nd floor without elevator is the softer minus. Carré des Antiquaires is one of the best micro-locations in the 7th.

listings.title: Family apartment, rue de Verneuil / Saint-Pères (Carré des Antiquaires)
condition_signals: original casement window with brass espagnolette handle
listings.notes: Same prized micro-location as 86131276 but larger, higher floor, lift. Against recent Paris 4th sales in the 80-250 m² surface band (n=57): median €12,858/m², IQR €10,171–€14,824/m². Asking €19,461/m² → **OVERPRICED** — Materially above the p75 — needs strong condition/floor/view justification.

Premium vs median: **+51.4%**.

**Strengths:** 1930 building (Pompidou), plein sud, Seine view.

**Concerns:** condition not visible from listing photo.

**Notes:** Quai de Béthune, building where Pompidou lived. Plein sud, vue dégagée. Only 1BR despite 102 m².
